steps.title: Identify what the question is asking
steps.content: The question asks which choice emphasizes how Wilkerson documents the Great Migration. The key word is how — the answer must describe her method of documentation, not just a fact about the book, an award, or a single person's story. The notes state: "To document this period, Wilkerson narrates the personal journeys of Ida Mae Gladney and George Swanson Starling, among others." Her method is building a historical account through individual narratives.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — Isabel Wilkerson chronicles the complex history of the Great Migration in such riveting, personal detail by narrating the journeys of individuals, such as Ida Mae Gladney and George Swanson Starling.: directly names her method — narrating individual journeys to build a complex historical account. ✓
  • Choice B — The personal journey of Ida Mae Gladney is featured in the award-winning book The Warmth of Other Suns.: focuses on a single featured person, not on Wilkerson's documentation approach. ✗
  • Choice C — In 2011, Isabel Wilkerson's book The Warmth of Other Suns won not only the Hurston/Wright Legacy Award but also the Hillman Prize.: mentions awards, which says nothing about how she documents the migration. ✗
  • Choice D — Los Angeles, California, is one of the cities whose African American populations grew during the Great Migration.: supplies historical context about the migration itself, not about Wilkerson's method. ✗
That is the correct answer.
